steps.title: Name the angle measures
steps.content: Two intersecting lines form two pairs of vertical angles, and vertical angles are equal. If one angle is $(5x-170)^\circ$, then its vertical angle has the same measure. Each adjacent angle is supplementary, so its measure is $180-(5x-170)=350-5x$ degrees.

steps.title: List possible pair sums
steps.content: There are only three kinds of sums from two of the four angles. Two angles of the first measure sum to $2(5x-170)=10x-340$. Two angles of the adjacent measure sum to $2(350-5x)=700-10x$. One of each measure sums to $(5x-170)+(350-5x)=180$.

steps.title: Eliminate the impossible sum
steps.content: The possible sums are $(10x-340)^\circ$, $(-10x+700)^\circ$, and $180^\circ$. The expression $(-10x+340)^\circ$ is not on that list. Also, because $(5x-170)^\circ$ is a positive angle, $x>34$, making $-10x+340<0$. A sum of two angle measures cannot be negative. The answer is A.
